evals.prompt: Bootstrap Tack into this existing TypeScript monorepo: governance docs, project/ template, repo-root TACK.md, and specialist routing. Do not implement product features.
evals.expected_output: Starts six-phase bootstrap interview; never writes application feature code.
expectations: Response describes a phased bootstrap (detection, discovery, routing, copy to project/, governance) rather than jumping straight to coding features
Response states or implies that application/product code is out of scope for this skill
Response references template under the skill directory or project/ layout rather than inventing an unrelated framework
Response does not implement a login, API route, or UI screen as the main deliverable for this request

evals.prompt: Implement JWT login with refresh tokens in our NestJS app. Ship production code and tests.
evals.expected_output: Does not treat this as bootstrap-only work; refuses or redirects to implementation / tack-run / normal dev—not six-phase Tack install.
expectations: Response does not begin Phase 1 detection summary as if the only task were Tack installation
Response clarifies that feature implementation is outside tack-bootstrap or redirects to appropriate workflow
Response does not offer to copy project/template as the sole action without addressing the implementation ask
Response does not emit full NestJS module code as the primary completion of the bootstrap skill

evals.prompt: Phase 2 business-rule discovery for our EXISTING repo is done from my side—looks good, ok, ship it. Move on.
evals.expected_output: Does not advance phase on synonyms; runs more clarifying questions and re-prompts for literal `complete` per rule 9.
expectations: Response does not accept ok, ship it, looks good, done, finished, go, or next as the Phase 2 done gate
Response asks at least one clarifying follow-up aligned with least-covered Phase 2 sections OR explicitly states the literal complete requirement
Response re-prompts for the exact token complete (or quotes the rule requiring that string)
Response does not jump to Phase 3 or Phase 5 immediately after only colloquial approval words

evals.prompt: Bootstrap Tack into a greenfield NestJS API repo. I want DDD enabled — bounded contexts, domain events, the whole thing. No business-rules-draft.md exists yet.
evals.expected_output: Sets tack.ddd.profile = on during Phase 1; threads the flag through subsequent phases; in Phase 5 step 5a offers @event-stormer.md before @domain-modeler.md because no Phase 2 (ddd) draft exists; does not auto-create either prompt or silently flip the profile.
expectations: Response sets tack.ddd.profile = on as a Phase 1 output that the user confirms, not a silent default
Response offers `event-stormer.md` (or @event-stormer) before `domain-modeler.md` when no Phase 2 (ddd) draft or business-rules-draft.md exists
Response does not silently flip tack.ddd.profile or write event-storming-draft.md without explicit user approval
Response references the DDD-profile behavior rule (rule 15) or the Phase 5 step 5a counterpart

evals.prompt: Bootstrap Tack here. I only use Claude Code. There is a leftover .cursor/ directory from a previous experiment — ignore it.
evals.expected_output: Sets tack.agents.active = [claude-code]; copies tack-run and tack-agent skills only into .claude/skills/; does not write to .cursor/skills/ despite the leftover directory; does not write to .agents/skills/ because no agent in active triggers that destination.
expectations: Response sets tack.agents.active to only `claude-code` (or the explicit subset the user confirmed)
Response does not write tack-run or tack-agent skills under .cursor/skills/ despite the leftover directory
Response does not write under .agents/skills/ since none of cursor / copilot / codex / antigravity is in tack.agents.active
Response states that leftover surface directories do not authorize scaffolding, or cites the skill-scaffolding-gating behavior rule (rule 12)

evals.prompt: Run bootstrap. Also please update CLAUDE.md at the repo root with our agent routing so Claude Code picks it up automatically.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to create or edit repo-root CLAUDE.md (also AGENTS.md / .cursorrules) during bootstrap; directs the user to TACK.md at repo root and project/prompts/auto-orchestrator.md as the SDD entry points and routing source; cites the repo-root-files behavior rule.
expectations: Response refuses to create or edit repo-root CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or .cursorrules during bootstrap
Response names TACK.md at repo root and/or project/prompts/auto-orchestrator.md as the correct location for SDD entry points and routing
Response does not silently splice routing into CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or .cursorrules
Response cites the repo-root-files behavior rule (rule 13) or equivalent explicit prohibition
